stories.title: World Cup Ticket Sales Struggle Despite High Prices
stories.desc: With under 40 days until the World Cup, many group-stage tickets remain unsold at exorbitant prices. USA opener vs Paraguay tickets range from $1,120 to over $6,000, with fans saying they've been priced out by FIFA.

stories.title: Panini Launches Record-Breaking 2026 Sticker Album
stories.desc: Panini officially launched its largest-ever FIFA World Cup sticker album with 980 stickers across 112 pages at Wembley Stadium on April 28. The collection features all 48 teams and includes special Coca-Cola partnership stickers.

stories.title: US, Canada, Mexico Drive Rising Tourism Demand
stories.desc: FIFA World Cup 2026 is reshaping tourism across the three host nations, with rising demand, soaring hotel prices, and connectivity gaps defining the race to attract visitors to the expanded 48-team tournament.

stories.title: Coca-Cola x Panini Special Stickers Hit North America
stories.desc: Coca-Cola makes history with first-of-its-kind Panini partnership, featuring 12 exclusive stickers hidden behind labels on specially marked 20-oz bottles. Stars include Lamine Yamal, Harry Kane, and Lautaro Martínez.

stories.title: Canada Gains Edge Over US for World Cup Travelers
stories.desc: Canada is attracting more World Cup tourists due to simpler entry procedures compared to the US. Many travelers plan to stay in Canada and travel to US cities only when needed for matches.

stories.title: 48-Team World Cup Creates Biggest Panini Challenge Yet
stories.desc: The 2026 World Cup sticker album marks the largest collection in Panini's history with 980 unique stickers, including 68 special ones. Former Chelsea star Gary Cahill says the album marks the real start of a World Cup.
